text: Root cause diagnosis is not proven — mcp-remote#113 is not a confirmed match. Three explanations remain live.
Native connectors may inherit the same chat-mode session problem if Desktop itself is the layer losing state.
Security implications understated — Bearer auth remains, OAuth adds 30-day refresh tokens, more credentials during Phase 1.
Phase 1 dual registration unsafe — duplicate mutating tools, session cap pressure, global rate limiter, uninterpretable logs.
onsessionclosed only deletes map entry but doesn't call server.close(), unlike evictSession() — real leak.
Stateless HTTP mode overlooked — SDK supports it, Munin fits the profile (tool-only, no per-session state), directly removes the failing requirement.
Prior stdio-bridge debate already converged on SDK-based bridge as fix for session bugs — not referenced.
Should this even be fixed? Current state (cowork works, chat doesn't) is acceptable as temporary limitation.
C4 pushback dodges the point — dual registration makes the experiment uninterpretable regardless of duration, since logs don't record connector/auth source.
Stateless mode is not just flipping sessionIdGenerator — SDK requires fresh transport per request in stateless mode. Implementation delta understated.
Rate limiter is already global (not session-based) — Claude's concern about losing session-based rate limiting in stateless mode was wrong.
Observability is weak even before stateless — logs omit auth mechanism, client identity, connector source.
